untranslatable_reason: EEDT (Even-legged Expanding Double Top) pattern detection requires visual/structural chart pattern recognition that cannot be expressed using only the allowed price and technical indicators. The signal relies on identifying a specific candlestick formation (double top with expanding legs) which requires pattern-matching logic beyond simple indicator comparisons. Additionally, the author provides no quantitative thresholds for stop placement, target prices, volume trends, or success rates in bull vs bear markets that could be operationalized.
translation_notes: Signal contains critical context-dependent logic: SHORT trades in bull markets have poor performance (63% average rise vs 19% average decline noted). Attempted to create a minimal short entry based on trend strength (ADX) and exit on bullish reversal (price above SMA_20), but this does not capture the EEDT pattern itself. The pattern recognition component is untranslatable without custom pattern detection logic. Recommend: (1) implement EEDT pattern scanner separately, (2) add regime filter to avoid shorts in TRENDING_UP markets, (3) define explicit stop/target rules from additional source material.
